title: C4OS Research Grill Intake
description: Answer these locally, submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat. This intake seeds the grill session; we will still challenge the answers one decision at a time.

questions.label: First MVP workflow
questions.help: Describe the smallest customer-usable workflow C4OS must prove first. Keep it concrete: who opens what, asks what, approves what, and sees what result.
questions.placeholder: Example: A developer opens one trusted repo, starts one session, asks for a small code change, reviews a proposed file edit/command, and resumes the session later.

questions.label: Concurrent runs in first MVP
options: Single active run only; defer concurrent runs
Multiple sessions visible, but only one active run at a time
Multiple concurrent runs are required in MVP
questions.help: Research currently flags concurrent runs as unresolved. The recommended default is one active run until approval/output isolation is proven.

questions.label: Runtime backend for first implementation
options: Pi first behind a thin C4OS adapter
OpenCode first behind a thin C4OS adapter
Keep both as proof backends behind one adapter
questions.help: POCs found both viable behind a C4OS-owned adapter. Pi proved in-process approval interception; OpenCode proved server-backed session/event control.

questions.label: Browser scope for first MVP
options: Full native Browser is required despite current proof risk
questions.help: Research currently rejects direct Tauri WebviewWindow for untrusted Browser content because page content saw Tauri internals.

questions.label: Terminal scope for first MVP
options: Full interactive Terminal is required despite current gating
questions.help: Terminal proofs support backend ownership on macOS; full interactive behavior is still gated on bridge scoping, audit/backpressure, and non-macOS validation.

questions.label: Extension inventory in first MVP
options: Install/connect flows are required in MVP
questions.help: Research currently treats extension execution as post-MVP unless the first workflow truly needs inventory visibility.

questions.label: Known blockers or objections
questions.help: List anything you already know would block freezing the MVP after the grill: missing proof, wrong scope, terminology confusion, UI concern, or business requirement.

questions.label: Context promotions you expect
options: Do not promote anything until after grill
questions.help: This does not perform promotion automatically. It tells me what you expect to become reusable context after decisions crystallize.
